page: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dlsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores with hyper-threading enabled at 2 threads per core. The system is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable. The processor features a large 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache.

In benchmark evaluations, the D32ls_v7 demonstrates strong performance in specific data processing and inference tasks. It achieves top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression at 35.73 GB/sec and single-threaded bzip3 decompression at 14.74 GB/sec, alongside strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 204.86 GB/sec. For artificial intelligence workloads, the server exhibits top-tier performance in text generation using a small 135M LLM model at 362.36 tokens/sec and strong performance in prompt processing for both 135M and 7B models. General CPU and memory benchmarks, including stress-ng, PassMark CPU, and Redis SET operations, consistently fall within the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32ls_v7 offers a streamlined hardware profile optimized for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints, making it a resource-dense option for raw processing power. However, the lack of local storage and a relatively low memory-to-vCPU ratio represent clear tradeoffs for memory-intensive databases or applications requiring high-speed local scratch disks. This server is highly suited for compute-bound tasks, web serving, single-threaded compression pipelines, and lightweight LLM inference workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. While it lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, alongside strong capabilities in lightweight LLM text generation and prompt processing. Conversely, standard multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks align with average mid-tier performance. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this server a cost-effective, resource-dense choice for compute-heavy tasks, web serving, and data compression pipelines that do not demand extensive memory allocations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v7 is a compute-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, and a 480 MB L3 cache, delivering top-tier performance in compression and lightweight LLM inference.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32ls_v7 is a compute-optimized VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and top-tier single-threaded compression performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and 64 GB of RAM for high-throughput compression and text generation workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with a 480 MB L3 cache
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory (2.0 GB per vCPU)
Top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression
Strong capabilities in lightweight LLM text generation and prompt processing
No local storage or bundled GPUs, but Premium Storage capable
Well-suited for compute-bound tasks, web serving, and data processing pipelines
